title: Ear fullness

red_flags.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
red_flags.rationale: The exact adult hearing section supports onset, hearing change, functional impact, associated or negative features, and clinician-performed ENT assessment without diagnosing wax, infection, middle-ear pressure, or inner-ear disease.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment

investigations.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
investigations.rationale: The exact audiology section supports concerns, reviewed audiology or tympanometry, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without ordering tests or treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

referral.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
referral.rationale: The exact signs section supports clinician concern and relevant persistent pain, discharge, asymmetry, tinnitus, or vertigo context without creating referral urgency.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source_limitations: Laterality, trigger, duration, hearing, tinnitus, vertigo, pain, discharge, pressure exposure, nasal symptoms, examination, audiology, tympanometry, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
